actions.description: Attacker deposits 1 MockToken (1e18 wei) into the SocketGateway, increasing sourceGateway.totalLocked.
Honest relay forwards the lock proof to the destination chain.
SocketGateway on destination mints 1 MockToken to the attacker, totalMinted = 1e18.
Attacker replays the same message ID, causing the relay to deliver a duplicate mint instruction.
Gateway mints a second 1 MockToken without a corresponding new lock, breaking the invariant.

waypoints.description: Attacker deposits 1 MockToken (1e18 wei) into the SocketGateway, increasing sourceGateway.totalLocked.
Honest relay forwards the lock proof to the destination chain.
SocketGateway on destination mints 1 MockToken to the attacker, totalMinted = 1e18.
Attacker replays the same message ID, causing the relay to deliver a duplicate mint instruction.
Gateway mints a second 1 MockToken without a corresponding new lock, breaking the invariant.

actions.description: Attacker approves the SocketGateway to pull 1,000 MockToken.
Gateway pulls 1,000 tokens, calculates a 30% fee (300 tokens), updates internal `feeCollected` += 300, and emits a relay message indicating 700 tokens should be minted.
Malicious relay rewrites the payload, reducing the mint amount from the honest 700 tokens to only 100 tokens while leaving the fee accounting untouched.
Destination gateway mints only 100 tokens to the attacker as instructed by the tampered relay.

waypoints.description: Attacker approves the SocketGateway to pull 1,000 MockToken.
Gateway pulls 1,000 tokens, calculates a 30% fee (300 tokens), updates internal `feeCollected` += 300, and emits a relay message indicating 700 tokens should be minted.
Malicious relay rewrites the payload, reducing the mint amount from the honest 700 tokens to only 100 tokens while leaving the fee accounting untouched.
Destination gateway mints only 100 tokens to the attacker as instructed by the tampered relay.

actions.description: Attacker grants unlimited allowance to the SocketGateway.
Attacker calls transferFrom with amount > attacker balance; MockToken lacks a balance >= amount check, causing the sender's balance to underflow into a negative value.
Gateway records the pull and emits the cross‑chain message.
Relay (faithful mode) delivers the mint call; attacker receives tokens on the destination chain while source balance is negative.

waypoints.description: Attacker grants unlimited allowance to the SocketGateway.
Attacker calls transferFrom with amount > attacker balance; MockToken lacks a balance >= amount check, causing the sender's balance to underflow into a negative value.
Gateway records the pull and emits the cross‑chain message.
Relay (faithful mode) delivers the mint call; attacker receives tokens on the destination chain while source balance is negative.

actions.description: Pull tokens into SocketGateway, locking them on the source chain.
Emit cross‑chain message to the relay.
Relay forwards the queued message to the destination chain.
Destination gateway mints the locked amount to the attacker.
Attacker re‑submits the same message after it has already been processed.
Second mint creates extra supply on the destination chain, breaking the asset‑conservation invariant.

waypoints.description: Pull tokens into SocketGateway, locking them on the source chain.
Emit cross‑chain message to the relay.
Relay forwards the queued message to the destination chain.
Destination gateway mints the locked amount to the attacker.
Attacker re‑submits the same message after it has already been processed.
Second mint creates extra supply on the destination chain, breaking the asset‑conservation invariant.

actions.function: performAction(uint32 actionId,address from,address to,uint256 amount,address token,address feeToken,bytes data)
actions.description: Attacker initiates a legitimate pull (e3) locking 1000 MockToken on the source chain. State predicate: sourceGateway.lockedAmountByNonce[nonce] == 1000e18.
Relay intercepts the cross‑chain message and inflates the amount field from 1000 to 1500 MockToken. State predicate: relayMessage.amount == 1500e18.
Destination gateway processes the tampered message and mints 1500 MockToken to the attacker (e4). State predicate: destGateway.mintRequestedAmount[nonce] == 1500e18.

waypoints.description: Attacker initiates a legitimate pull (e3) locking 1000 MockToken on the source chain. State predicate: sourceGateway.lockedAmountByNonce[nonce] == 1000e18.
Relay intercepts the cross‑chain message and inflates the amount field from 1000 to 1500 MockToken. State predicate: relayMessage.amount == 1500e18.
Destination gateway processes the tampered message and mints 1500 MockToken to the attacker (e4). State predicate: destGateway.mintRequestedAmount[nonce] == 1500e18.

actions.description: Attacker, who has compromised the ProxyAdmin (e.g., via a compromised relayer key), upgrades the SocketGateway proxy to a malicious implementation that skips relay verification before mint.
Attacker initiates a cross‑chain transfer. The payload is empty because the malicious implementation will ignore the relay verification step.
The malicious implementation of SocketGateway on Polygon executes the mint without checking `relayVerified[messageHash]`. This directly violates the invariant.
Attacker moves the freshly minted tokens to a wallet they control, realizing the economic gain.

waypoints.description: Attacker, who has compromised the ProxyAdmin (e.g., via a compromised relayer key), upgrades the SocketGateway proxy to a malicious implementation that skips relay verification before mint.
Attacker initiates a cross‑chain transfer. The payload is empty because the malicious implementation will ignore the relay verification step.
The malicious implementation of SocketGateway on Polygon executes the mint without checking `relayVerified[messageHash]`. This directly violates the invariant.
Attacker moves the freshly minted tokens to a wallet they control, realizing the economic gain.

actions.description: Attacker calls the gateway's entry point, supplying victim as the source user. The gateway internally invokes the `pull` edge (e3) without performing a proper ERC‑20 allowance check because the condition list is corrupted.
The Socket relay forwards the cross‑chain payload to the destination chain in a faithful mode (no tampering).
Upon receipt, the destination gateway mints the wrapped token to the attacker, completing the bridge transfer.

waypoints.description: Attacker calls the gateway's entry point, supplying victim as the source user. The gateway internally invokes the `pull` edge (e3) without performing a proper ERC‑20 allowance check because the condition list is corrupted.
The Socket relay forwards the cross‑chain payload to the destination chain in a faithful mode (no tampering).
Upon receipt, the destination gateway mints the wrapped token to the attacker, completing the bridge transfer.

actions.description: Victim (0xVictim) grants a large allowance to the attacker‑controlled contract.
Confirm victim holds a small balance that is insufficient for the upcoming transfer.
Attacker contract invokes MockToken.transferFrom using the previously approved allowance, requesting an amount far larger than the victim's balance.
Underlying ERC‑20 call; the buggy MockToken implementation only checks allowance, not balance, so the call succeeds.

waypoints.description: Victim (0xVictim) grants a large allowance to the attacker‑controlled contract.
Confirm victim holds a small balance that is insufficient for the upcoming transfer.
Attacker contract invokes MockToken.transferFrom using the previously approved allowance, requesting an amount far larger than the victim's balance.
Underlying ERC‑20 call; the buggy MockToken implementation only checks allowance, not balance, so the call succeeds.

actions.description: Deploy a malicious relay contract and register it as a trusted relayer for the Socket bridge. The contract stores the gateway address but will later forward messages with a tampered `msg.sender`.

actions.function: initiateBridge(uint256 amount, address dstToken, address dstRecipient)
actions.description: User calls `SocketGateway.performAction` (via the public entry point) specifying the malicious relay as the executor. The gateway emits a cross‑chain message that the AttackerRelay will consume.

actions.function: executeMint(address token, address to, uint256 amount)
actions.description: The registered relay receives the message and, instead of calling the gateway, directly invokes `MockToken.mint(to, amount)`. Because the call originates from the relay, `msg.sender` is the relay address, not `SocketGateway`, violating the invariant.
Verify that the attacker’s balance increased, confirming that mint succeeded without the gateway check.

waypoints.description: Deploy a malicious relay contract and register it as a trusted relayer for the Socket bridge. The contract stores the gateway address but will later forward messages with a tampered `msg.sender`.
User calls `SocketGateway.performAction` (via the public entry point) specifying the malicious relay as the executor. The gateway emits a cross‑chain message that the AttackerRelay will consume.

The registered relay receives the message and, instead of calling the gateway, directly invokes `MockToken.mint(to, amount)`. Because the call originates from the relay, `msg.sender` is the relay address, not `SocketGateway`, violating the invariant.
Verify that the attacker’s balance increased, confirming that mint succeeded without the gateway check.

Submit forged signature bundle to destination

Submit forged signature bundle to destination

actions.function: performAction(uint32 nonce,address from,address to,uint256 amount,address fromToken,address toToken,bytes data)
actions.description: User initiates cross‑chain transfer; gateway records nonce 42 as used on source chain.
Relay forwards the proof to the destination chain in faithful mode (first delivery).
Attacker replays the identical proof before the destination gateway has cleared the pending state.
Gateway processes the first proof, mints tokens to recipient and (incorrectly) does NOT mark nonce 42 as used on destination chain.

params.proof: <same abi‑encoded proof of performAction with nonce 42>

actions.description: Gateway processes the replayed proof, minting a second batch of tokens because nonce check is missing, violating uniqueness invariant.

waypoints.description: User initiates cross‑chain transfer; gateway records nonce 42 as used on source chain.
Relay forwards the proof to the destination chain in faithful mode (first delivery).
Attacker replays the identical proof before the destination gateway has cleared the pending state.
Gateway processes the first proof, mints tokens to recipient and (incorrectly) does NOT mark nonce 42 as used on destination chain.
Gateway processes the replayed proof, minting a second batch of tokens because nonce check is missing, violating uniqueness invariant.

actions.description: User (attacker) initiates a cross‑chain transfer. The gateway emits a signed relay message whose hash H is stored in `processedMessage[H] == false`.
Honest relayer forwards the signed message to the destination chain.
Destination gateway validates the message, sees `processedMessage[H] == false`, mints `amount` of MockToken to the attacker and sets `processedMessage[H] = true`.

Attacker re‑broadcasts the same message hash H after a short delay. The relay does not modify the payload, but the destination gateway contains a bug: the `processedMessage` mapping is cleared on each epoch rollover via `epochCleanup()` which the attacker triggers by calling `setCurrentEpoch(uint256)` on the gateway (owner‑only, but the owner key was compromised in a prior key‑compromise incident). The attacker first calls the compromised owner function to reset the epoch, which implicitly deletes the old `processedMessage[H]` entry, then replays the message.
Gateway processes the replayed message again, sees `processedMessage[H] == false` (because it was cleared), mints a second tranche of tokens to the attacker, violating the uniqueness invariant.

waypoints.description: User (attacker) initiates a cross‑chain transfer. The gateway emits a signed relay message whose hash H is stored in `processedMessage[H] == false`.
Honest relayer forwards the signed message to the destination chain.
Destination gateway validates the message, sees `processedMessage[H] == false`, mints `amount` of MockToken to the attacker and sets `processedMessage[H] = true`.

Attacker re‑broadcasts the same message hash H after a short delay. The relay does not modify the payload, but the destination gateway contains a bug: the `processedMessage` mapping is cleared on each epoch rollover via `epochCleanup()` which the attacker triggers by calling `setCurrentEpoch(uint256)` on the gateway (owner‑only, but the owner key was compromised in a prior key‑compromise incident). The attacker first calls the compromised owner function to reset the epoch, which implicitly deletes the old `processedMessage[H]` entry, then replays the message.
Gateway processes the replayed message again, sees `processedMessage[H] == false` (because it was cleared), mints a second tranche of tokens to the attacker, violating the uniqueness invariant.

actions.description: User approves and initiates bridge transfer; MockToken records nonceUsed[0xA1B2C3] = true and locks 1000 tokens.
Gateway emits a cross‑chain message containing the nonce 0xA1B2C3.
Honest relay forwards the message to the destination chain.
Destination gateway mints 1000 tokens for the recipient. The gateway **fails** to check `nonceUsed` on the source side.
Attacker re‑broadcasts the same message with the same nonce. Relay is configured (or compromised) to allow duplicate delivery.
Destination gateway mints again because it does not verify that `nonceUsed[0xA1B2C3]` is already true on the source chain, violating the invariant.

waypoints.description: User approves and initiates bridge transfer; MockToken records nonceUsed[0xA1B2C3] = true and locks 1000 tokens.
Gateway emits a cross‑chain message containing the nonce 0xA1B2C3.
Honest relay forwards the message to the destination chain.
Destination gateway mints 1000 tokens for the recipient. The gateway **fails** to check `nonceUsed` on the source side.
Attacker re‑broadcasts the same message with the same nonce. Relay is configured (or compromised) to allow duplicate delivery.
Destination gateway mints again because it does not verify that `nonceUsed[0xA1B2C3]` is already true on the source chain, violating the invariant.

actions.description: User A locks 1 MockToken on the source chain, creating nonce N. State predicate: `sourceGateway.lockedAmount(N) == 1e18 && sourceGateway.lockTimestamp(N) == block.timestamp`.
Malicious relay withholds the message for > REFUND_TIMEOUT, causing the destination mint to be absent when the timeout expires.

Attacker, masquerading as an authorized relayer, writes `minted[N] = true` on the source contract without any corresponding mint on the destination chain. State predicate: `sourceGateway.minted(N) == true`.

User A attempts to call `refund(N)` after timeout, but the call reverts because `minted[N]` is already true. Predicate: `block.timestamp >= lockTimestamp(N) + REFUND_TIMEOUT && minted(N) == true` → refund unavailable.

Attacker triggers the mint on the destination chain using the same nonce, receiving the locked tokens that were never actually minted for the legitimate user. State predicate: `MockToken.balanceOf(0xAttacker) == 1e18`.

waypoints.description: User A locks 1 MockToken on the source chain, creating nonce N. State predicate: `sourceGateway.lockedAmount(N) == 1e18 && sourceGateway.lockTimestamp(N) == block.timestamp`.
Malicious relay withholds the message for > REFUND_TIMEOUT, causing the destination mint to be absent when the timeout expires.

Attacker, masquerading as an authorized relayer, writes `minted[N] = true` on the source contract without any corresponding mint on the destination chain. State predicate: `sourceGateway.minted(N) == true`.

User A attempts to call `refund(N)` after timeout, but the call reverts because `minted[N]` is already true. Predicate: `block.timestamp >= lockTimestamp(N) + REFUND_TIMEOUT && minted(N) == true` → refund unavailable.

Attacker triggers the mint on the destination chain using the same nonce, receiving the locked tokens that were never actually minted for the legitimate user. State predicate: `MockToken.balanceOf(0xAttacker) == 1e18`.

actions.description: User locks 1,000 MockToken on the source chain, creating a pending outbound with nonce 42.
Attacker (or the same user) calls the refund path before the destination mint is processed; the gateway marks refunded[42]=true and releases the locked tokens.
A malicious relay re‑sends the original mint instruction after the refund, using replay mode to bypass any timeout validation performed by the relay.
Destination token contract mints 1,000 tokens for the user even though refunded[42] is true, violating the invariant.

waypoints.description: User locks 1,000 MockToken on the source chain, creating a pending outbound with nonce 42.
Attacker (or the same user) calls the refund path before the destination mint is processed; the gateway marks refunded[42]=true and releases the locked tokens.
A malicious relay re‑sends the original mint instruction after the refund, using replay mode to bypass any timeout validation performed by the relay.
Destination token contract mints 1,000 tokens for the user even though refunded[42] is true, violating the invariant.

actions.description: Attacker locks 1 MockToken on the source chain; SocketGateway records lockTimestamp[1] = block.timestamp (≈2000).
Honest relay forwards the lock message to the destination chain.
Destination gateway processes the message and sets lockTimestamp[1] = 2000.
Attacker tampers with the relayed payload, lowering lockTimestamp to 1500, and replays the same nonce after it has already been processed.
Destination gateway accepts the replayed, tampered message and overwrites lockTimestamp[1] with 1500, violating the monotonicity invariant.

waypoints.description: Attacker locks 1 MockToken on the source chain; SocketGateway records lockTimestamp[1] = block.timestamp (≈2000).
Honest relay forwards the lock message to the destination chain.
Destination gateway processes the message and sets lockTimestamp[1] = 2000.
Attacker tampers with the relayed payload, lowering lockTimestamp to 1500, and replays the same nonce after it has already been processed.
Destination gateway accepts the replayed, tampered message and overwrites lockTimestamp[1] with 1500, violating the monotonicity invariant.

actions.description: Attacker initiates a lock of 1,000 MockToken on the source chain, creating nonce 42. This writes lockTimestamp[42] = block.timestamp and minted[42] = false.

Relay forwards a mint message to the destination chain but manipulates the payload (mode = tampered_skip_timeout_clear) so that SocketGateway calls MockToken.mint without invoking the internal timeout‑reset hook. minted[42] becomes true while lockTimestamp[42] remains non‑zero.

After the timeout period has elapsed, attacker calls refund on the source bridge. Because lockTimestamp[42] was never cleared, the contract’s refund logic (which only checks timeout) succeeds, transferring the locked tokens back to the attacker and setting refunded[42] = true.

waypoints.description: Attacker initiates a lock of 1,000 MockToken on the source chain, creating nonce 42. This writes lockTimestamp[42] = block.timestamp and minted[42] = false.

Relay forwards a mint message to the destination chain but manipulates the payload (mode = tampered_skip_timeout_clear) so that SocketGateway calls MockToken.mint without invoking the internal timeout‑reset hook. minted[42] becomes true while lockTimestamp[42] remains non‑zero.

After the timeout period has elapsed, attacker calls refund on the source bridge. Because lockTimestamp[42] was never cleared, the contract’s refund logic (which only checks timeout) succeeds, transferring the locked tokens back to the attacker and setting refunded[42] = true.

actions.description: Lock MockToken on source chain and emit a relay message H with a 5‑minute deadline.
Relay node rewrites the deadline field of message H before signing and forwards it.
Gateway on destination chain processes the tampered message and invokes mint.
Mint an inflated amount of MockToken despite the original deadline having passed.

waypoints.description: Lock MockToken on source chain and emit a relay message H with a 5‑minute deadline.
Relay node rewrites the deadline field of message H before signing and forwards it.
Gateway on destination chain processes the tampered message and invokes mint.
Mint an inflated amount of MockToken despite the original deadline having passed.
